---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    logo: zicklin-logo.JPG
    footer: "Zicklin School of Business | Student Ambassador Virtual Cafe | April 15, 2026"
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.1
    center: false
    controls: true
    controls-layout: bottom-right
    touch: true
---

## {background-color="#003087"}

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; height:85%; align-items:center; gap:2rem; padding:1rem 2rem;">

<div style="color:white;">
<div style="font-size:0.75em; color:#CFB87C; letter-spacing:3px; text-transform:uppercase; margin-bottom:1rem;">Baruch College - Zicklin School of Business</div>
<div style="font-size:2em; font-weight:700; line-height:1.2; margin-bottom:1rem;">Productivity Without Burnout</div>
<div style="font-size:1.1em; color:#CFB87C; margin-bottom:1.5rem;">Thriving in Graduate School</div>
<div style="font-size:0.8em; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.75); border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.25); padding-top:1rem;">
Wednesday, April 15 &bull; 10:30 AM to 11:30 AM<br/>Student Ambassador Virtual Cafe
</div>
</div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:1.5rem;">
<img src="zicklin-logo.JPG" style="width:200px; background:white; padding:1rem; border-radius:12px; box-shadow:0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);"/>
<div style="background:rgba(207,184,124,0.15); border:1px solid #CFB87C; border-radius:10px; padding:1.2rem; text-align:center; color:white; font-size:0.85em; line-height:1.6;">
A conversation about working smart,<br/>resting well, and finishing strong.
</div>
</div>

</div>
```

---

## About Me - Raúl {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="10%"}
<div style="width:220px; height:220px; border-radius:50%; overflow:hidden; border:4px solid #003087; margin:0 auto;"><img src="raul-headshot.jpg" style="width:230px; height:250px; max-width:none; max-height:none; margin-top:-20px;"/></div>

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
**Raúl J. Solá Navarro**

Student Ambassador

Expected Graduation Fall 2026
:::
:::
::: {.column width="70%"}
**Tell us about yourself!**

- Master in Science in Business Analytics with a concentration in Data Analytics.
- Former [Texas Instruments](https://www.ti.com/about-ti/company/overview.html) Finance & Operations Leader and Founder and Owner at [IronSportz](https://www.linkedin.com/company/iron-sportz/)
- To me "burnout" is a feeling of overwhelment which causes lost of motivation. I want to help other students to cope with this feeling and share the strategies that helped me to overcome it.

::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-left: 4px solid #003087; padding: 0.8rem; margin-top: 1rem; border-radius: 4px; font-style: italic;"}
Tip: When feeling stuck, try completing the easiest task to build some momentum.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Introduce yourself -- program, year, why you care about this topic personally.
:::

---

## About Me - May {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="10%"}
<div style="width:220px; height:220px; border-radius:50%; overflow:hidden; border:4px solid #003087; margin:0 auto;"><img src="may-headshot.jpg" style="width:250px; height:240px;
max-width:none; max-height:none; margin-top: -10px;"/></div>

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
**May Phyo Paing**

Student Ambassador

Expected Graduation Fall 2026
:::
:::
::: {.column width="70%"}
**Tell us about yourself!**

- Master of Science in Finance (General concentration) 
- Former Senior Analyst at [SMBC](https://www.smfg.co.jp/english/); current Graduate TA at [Baruch College](https://www.baruch.cuny.edu/) and Finance Intern at [Dakdan](http://www.dakdan.com)
- To me, 'burnout' is the exhaustion that sets in when coursework, job hunting, and 
personal responsibilities all collide. I want to help fellow students recognize 
this feeling early on and share the practical strategies that keep me balanced.

::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-left: 4px solid #003087; padding: 0.8rem; margin-top: 1rem; border-radius: 4px; font-style: italic;"}
Tip: When feeling overwhelmed, hit pause. Do a quick 'brain dump' of every pending task, set clear deadlines, and prioritize what actually needs your attention first. A clear plan instantly reduces stress and makes the workload manageable.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Introduce yourself -- program, year, your personal story.
:::

---

## Lets Meet Everyone! {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 2rem;"}

Please drop this in the **chat**

::: {style="background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.5rem; margin: 1rem auto; max-width: 600px; font-size: 1.1em;"}
**Your name - Your program - One emoji that describes your stress level right now**
:::

We will read a few out loud!

:::

---

## Today's Agenda {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 1rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**01**

The ROI of Effort
:::
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**02**

Career Hustle Boundaries
:::
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**03**

Strategic Collaboration
:::
::: {style="background: #CFB87C; color: #003087; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**04**

Intentional Rest
:::
::: {style="background: #CFB87C; color: #003087; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**05**

Red Flags and Early Wins
:::
::: {style="background: #1a5276; color: white; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.2rem; text-align: center;"}
**06**

Q and A + Connect
:::
:::

---

## 01 - The Art of Prioritization {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
The ROI of Effort Framework
:::

---

## Not All Tasks Deserve Equal Effort {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.2rem; font-size: 0.9em;"}
A 2019 study in the Journal of Experimental Psychology found that people who distinguish between high- and low-stakes tasks report **31% lower stress** and higher academic performance.
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**High-Impact Work**

- Coursework tied to your specialization
- Genuine recruiter and professional relationships
- Projects with real deliverables
- Strategic rest and recovery
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Busy Work**

- Perfecting formatting no one reads
- Attending every optional event
- Re-reading notes you already know
- Refreshing email every 10 minutes
:::
:::

---

## The Eisenhower Matrix {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.5rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="background: #d4edda; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #28a745;"}
**DO NOW**

Urgent and Important

Exam tomorrow - Deadline today - Health issue
:::
::: {style="background: #cce5ff; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #004085;"}
**SCHEDULE**

Not Urgent and Important

Career goals - Relationships - Self-care - Planning
:::
::: {style="background: #fff3cd; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #856404;"}
**DELEGATE**

Urgent and Not Important

Some emails - Routine admin - Certain meetings
:::
::: {style="background: #f8d7da; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #842029;"}
**ELIMINATE**

Not Urgent and Not Important

Social media spirals - Unnecessary perfecting
:::
:::

::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-left: 4px solid #003087; padding: 0.7rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.85em;"}
**The Grad School Trap**: Most burnout comes from spending time in the Delegate and Eliminate boxes, not from actually being too busy.
:::

---

## The Good Enough Permission Slip {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
Research by Dr. Barry Schwartz shows that "satisficers" -- people who choose good enough -- report higher life satisfaction than "maximizers" who over-optimize.

**Graduate School Translation**

| Quality | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 90% paper you submit | Gets graded |
| 100% paper never turned in | F |
| Weekly networking emails | Career connections |
| Perfect emails never sent | Silence |
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
::: {style="background: white; border: 3px dashed #003087; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.5rem; text-align: center;"}
**Permission Slip**

I give myself permission to do this task at:

**Good Enough**

Signed -- Future-Me Who Slept Last Night
:::
:::
:::

---

## 02 - Compartmentalizing the Career Hustle {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
Recruiting Without Losing Your Mind
:::

---

## Raise Your Hand {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 2.5rem; background: white; border-radius: 16px; border: 3px solid #003087; max-width: 700px; margin: 1rem auto;"}
**Raise your hand or type YES in chat**

Have you ever felt guilty for NOT job searching?

::: {style="margin-top: 1.5rem; padding: 1rem; background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 8px;"}
We see you. We have been there.

**Lets fix that.**
:::
:::

---

## Time Blocking - The Career Hustle Fix {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="54%"}
**Why Recruiting Takes Over**

- Job search is **infinite** -- there is always one more application, company, or connection
- Without structure, recruiting anxiety bleeds into every moment
- Cal Newport in Deep Work -- The pull to always be doing something kills deep focus

**The Fix**

Reserve **specific hours** for job search. Outside those hours -- it does not exist.

> Done for today is not giving up. It is protecting tomorrow's energy.
:::
::: {.column width="46%"}
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem;"}
**Sample Weekly Block**

<table style='width:100%; color:white; font-size:0.85em; border-collapse:collapse;'>
<tr style='background:rgba(255,255,255,0.2);'><th style='padding:0.4rem;'>Day</th><th style='padding:0.4rem;'>Block</th><th style='padding:0.4rem;'>Task</th></tr>
<tr style='background:#003087 !important;'><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>Mon</td><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>7-8pm</td><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>Applications</td></tr>
<tr style='background:rgba(255,255,255,0.1);'><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>Wed</td><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>12-1pm</td><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>Networking</td></tr>
<tr style='background:#003087 !important;'><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>Fri</td><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>6-7pm</td><td style='padding:0.4rem; color:white !important; background:#003087 !important;'>Follow-up</td></tr>
<tr style='background:rgba(255,255,255,0.1);'><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>Weekend</td><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>Off</td><td style='padding:0.4rem;'>Full rest</td></tr>
</table>

About 3 hrs/week of focused effort vs. 20hrs of anxious scrolling










:::
:::
:::

---

## School Brain vs. Career Brain {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; height: 100%;"}
**School Brain**

When -- Class, studying, group projects

Mindset -- Learner, analyst, collaborator

Tools -- Canvas, notes, textbooks

Off trigger -- Close the career portal when you open your textbook
:::
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="background: #fff8e1; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; height: 100%;"}
**Career Brain**

When -- Your designated recruiting blocks

Mindset -- Marketer, relationship-builder

Tools -- LinkedIn, resume, email

Off trigger -- One tab rule -- close Handshake when you open Canvas
:::
:::
:::

::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-left: 4px solid #003087; padding: 0.7rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.85em;"}
**Neuroscience**: Context switching costs about 23 minutes of refocus time (Gloria Mark, UC Irvine). Protecting brain modes is not just strategy -- it is science.
:::

---

## 03 - The Power of Strategic Collaboration {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
Working Smarter Together
:::

---

## Quick Poll - Who Are You in Group Projects? {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="text-align: center; background: white; border: 3px solid #003087; border-radius: 16px; padding: 2rem; max-width: 680px; margin: 0 auto;"}
**Type your letter in the chat!**

::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin-top: 1rem; text-align: left;"}
::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem;"}
**A -- The Planner**

Makes the timeline nobody follows
:::
::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem;"}
**B -- The Executor**

Actually builds the thing
:::
::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem;"}
**C -- The Presenter**

Makes it look good to the world
:::
::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem;"}
**D -- The Fixer**

Cleans up at 2am
:::
:::
:::

---

## Divide by Energy, Not Just Skill {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; font-size: 0.9em;"}
Teams that align tasks with members energy patterns outperform skill-matched teams by **up to 20%** on complex projects (Journal of Applied Psychology, 2014)
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Old Way -- Divide by Skill**

- "You are good at Excel -- you do the model"
- "You speak well -- you present"
- Results in resentment and hidden imbalance
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Better Way -- Divide by Energy**

- Who feels energized doing deep analysis?
- Who works best in the morning vs. evening?
- Who actually enjoys writing vs. visuals?
:::
:::

::: {style="background: #CFB87C; color: #003087; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem; margin-top: 1rem; font-weight: 500;"}
**Try this**: Start every group project with 10 minutes of everyone completing: I do my best work when... -- it changes the dynamic completely.
:::

---

## Avoiding the Team Carry Trap {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
**Why You Carry the Team**

- Fear of bad grades you cannot control
- Conflict avoidance
- Perfectionism
- I am always the reliable one

**Why You Should Stop**

- Breeds resentment in you
- Robs teammates of learning
- Accelerates your burnout fast
- Trains them to keep relying on you

**The Redistribution Script**

> "I want to make sure we all own a piece of this -- can we each commit to X by Thursday?"
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
::: {style="background: #f8d7da; border: 2px solid #842029; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem;"}
**Warning Signs**

- You know everyone's tasks better than your own
- You are the only one checking the group chat
- You have done it yourself more than twice
- You feel anxious when others handle their parts
- You rewrite others work silently
:::
:::
:::

---

## 04 - Normalizing Unproductive Rest {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
Your Brain Needs Downtime to Perform
:::

---

## Rest is Maintenance - The Science {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**What Research Actually Shows**

- The brain's default mode network activates during rest -- this is where creativity, insight, and memory consolidation happen (Buckner et al., 2008)

- Students who take regular breaks retain **30% more** than those who study continuously (Kornell and Bjork, 2008)

- Sleep deprivation reduces cognitive performance to the equivalent of being **legally drunk** (Williamson and Feyer, 2000)

- A 10-minute walk **doubles creative output** in the subsequent task (Stanford, 2014)
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem;"}
**Types of Intentional Rest**

**Micro-rest**
5-min break every 50 min (Pomodoro)

**Active rest**
Walk, stretch, cook (not scrolling)

**Social rest**
Time with people who energize you

**Sleep**
7-9 hours. Non-negotiable.

**Mind-wandering**
Let your brain process in the background
:::
:::
:::

---

## Intentional Rest vs. Avoidance {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="background: #d4edda; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #28a745;"}
**Intentional Rest**

- **Chosen** deliberately
- Has a defined **start and end**
- Leaves you feeling **recharged**
- Examples -- scheduled walk, dinner with friends, reading fiction, nap with alarm

Rest is giving yourself permission to stop -- so you can go again.
:::
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="background: #f8d7da; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #842029;"}
**Avoidance (Rest Disguise)**

- You **fell into it** to escape
- Feels **slightly guilty** the whole time
- Leaves you **more anxious** than before
- Examples -- 3-hour TikTok spiral, Netflix when you should be working

Avoidance does not restore -- it just delays.
:::
:::
:::

::: {style="background: #e8f0fe; border-left: 4px solid #003087; padding: 0.7rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
**The tell**: After intentional rest you feel **ready**. After avoidance you feel **worse**. Use this as your compass.
:::

---

## 05 - Recognizing Red Flags Early {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 2rem; text-align: center;"}
Burnout Does Not Arrive Overnight
:::

---

## The Burnout Warning System {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 1rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="background: #fff3cd; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #856404;"}
**Emotional**

- Constant irritability
- Dread before class or work
- Emotional numbness
- Cynicism about your program
- Feeling like nothing matters
:::
::: {style="background: #f8d7da; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #842029;"}
**Physical**

- Chronic fatigue even after sleep
- Frequent headaches
- Getting sick more often
- Insomnia or oversleeping
- Appetite changes
:::
::: {style="background: #cce5ff; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem; border-top: 4px solid #004085;"}
**Academic**

- Falling behind with no catch-up plan
- Procrastination becoming the norm
- Confusion on topics you once knew
- Missing deadlines for the first time
- Avoiding professors and advisors
:::
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.85em; color: #555; margin-top: 0.8rem; text-align: center;"}
Based on Maslach Burnout Inventory dimensions: Emotional Exhaustion, De-personalization, Reduced Personal Accomplishment
:::

---

## The Traffic Light Check-In {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Every Sunday, Rate Yourself**

::: {style="display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.7rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="background: #d4edda; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem;"}
**Green** -- Capable, rested, engaged. Keep going.
:::
::: {style="background: #fff3cd; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem;"}
**Yellow** -- Something is off. Managing, but barely.
:::
::: {style="background: #f8d7da; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem;"}
**Red** -- In distress. Need to act today.
:::
:::

**If You Are Yellow or Red**

1. Name what is specifically overwhelming you
2. Cancel one non-essential commitment
3. Talk to one person (friend, mentor, advisor)
4. Access Baruch Counseling Center -- it is free
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {style="background: #003087; color: white; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.2rem;"}
**Baruch Resources**

* [Counseling Center](https://studentaffairs.baruch.cuny.edu/counseling/)

* [Graduate Career Management Center (GCMC)](https://zicklin.baruch.cuny.edu/academic-programs/graduate/student-experience/gcmc-graduate-career-management-center/)

* [Student Affairs](https://studentaffairs.baruch.cuny.edu/)

* [Baruch Learning Community -- Academic support and tutoring (SACC)](https://sacc.baruch.cuny.edu/)

* [Graduate Advisor](https://zicklin.baruch.cuny.edu/current/graduate-advisement-hub/)

They are on your side -- reach out! You do not have to be in crisis to ask for help.
:::
:::
:::

---

## The 5-Minute Weekly Reset {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
**A Simple Sunday Ritual**

Answer these 5 questions every week

1. **What was my biggest win this week?**
2. **What drained me the most?**
3. **What is the ONE most important thing next week?**
4. **Am I taking care of my body?** (sleep, food, movement)
5. **Do I owe anyone a conversation?**

Then write your **top 3 priorities** for the week on a sticky note. Put it where you will see it.
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
::: {style="background: #003087; color: #CFB87C; border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.5rem; text-align: center; font-size: 1.05em;"}
You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.

::: {style="color: white; margin-top: 0.8rem; font-size: 0.85em;"}
-- James Clear, Atomic Habits
:::
:::

::: {style="margin-top: 1rem; background: #e8f0fe; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem; font-size: 0.85em;"}
**Recommended tools**

- Calendar time blocking 
- Screen Time iPhone App 
- Accountability Buddy 
- Microsoft To Do List 
- Physical planner 
- Focus music playlist
:::
:::
:::

---

## Toolkit Recap {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 0.8rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #003087; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Eisenhower Matrix**

Prioritize by urgency x importance
:::
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #003087; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Pomodoro Technique**

25 on / 5 off / 15 after 4 cycles
:::
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #003087; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Time Blocking**

Tasks in calendar slots, not lists
:::
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #CFB87C; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Energy Audit**

Track what energizes vs. drains you
:::
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #CFB87C; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Traffic Light Check-In**

Weekly Green / Yellow / Red
:::
::: {style="background: white; border: 2px solid #CFB87C; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
**Good Enough Practice**

Set quality targets before you start
:::
:::

---

## Thank You! {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: #CFB87C; font-size: 1.4em; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}
Lets Stay Connected
:::

---

## Connect and Wrap Up {background-color="#f5f7fa"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}
**Find Us on LinkedIn**

[**May Phyo Paing**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/may-phyo-2829-paing/)

[**Raúl J. Solá Navarro**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/raul-sola-navarro/)

**Before You Go**

Please complete the **session feedback survey** -- link is in the chat!

Takes 2 minutes and helps make future sessions even better.

**Session Survey: [Link](https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/forms.gle/5kvnGKcT1pLjTLn39__;!!NM37aKQmapIJ!fhoZv5ORLN9Nbw4xMj7b_2ORjsTtHKb6f99L8IVpe8Wy4jI_BJzamv4eIJGEk3E4F8Hv_sdfu9k49UbbQzLtQudKJ750QeB3wsCff0EH$)**
:::
::: {.column width="52%"}
**Key Takeaways**

1. Prioritize by **impact**, not urgency
2. Set **boundaries** around recruiting time
3. Collaborate by **energy**, not just skill
4. Rest is **productive** -- protect it actively
5. Catch burnout **early** -- do not wait for red

::: {style="background: #003087; color: #CFB87C; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; margin-top: 1rem; text-align: center;"}
Graduate school is a sprint disguised as a marathon. Pace yourself.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Questions and Answers {background-color="#003087"}

::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 2rem;"}

**Questions? Thoughts? Reactions?**

::: {style="background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); border-radius: 12px; padding: 1.5rem; margin: 1rem auto; max-width: 600px;"}
Drop them in the **chat** or unmute and share!

Any questions we cannot answer today will be followed up by our faculty sponsor.
:::

::: {style="margin-top: 1.5rem; color: #CFB87C;"}
Thank you for spending your Wednesday morning with us!
:::
:::
::: {style="position: absolute; bottom: 2rem; left: 1rem; right: 5rem; font-size: 0.5em; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2); padding-top: 0.5rem;"}
AI Usage Statement -- Claude (Anthropic) was used in the making of this presentation to help summarize research findings, generate design ideas, and draft speaker notes. All content was reviewed and edited by the presenters to ensure accuracy and relevance.
:::
